terms.definition: The built-in JavaScript API for HTTP requests; returns a Promise that resolves to a Response.
The object returned by fetch, exposing status, ok, and body readers like json() and text().
A boolean that is true for 2xx HTTP statuses; used to detect HTTP errors fetch won't reject on.
An HTTP header carrying an API token, e.g. 'Bearer <key>', used to authenticate API requests.
A controller whose signal can cancel a fetch, enabling timeouts and cancellation.
The streaming body of a Response, read chunk-by-chunk for live/streamed output.
